step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is extracted directly from the whiteboard diagram and transcript. We define the user as UserCompanyDeveloper who inputs scripts. The Scheduler (EC2) places jobs onto the Job Queue (SQS). Public Monitors (EC2) pull from the Job Queue and test the Public Endpoint (ThirdParty). Private Monitors (EC2) route through Global Accelerator to the Monitor API (EC2) to fetch jobs and report metrics, performing internal tests on the Private Endpoint (ThirdParty). Results are fed to the Results Queue (SQS) to complete the feedback loop.

graph.name: New Relic Synthetics Private and Public Monitoring
graph.notes: Automated synthetic testing architecture using public and private minions to run scripted tests against endpoints securely.

nodes.name: Developer / CI/CD
nodes.notes: Submits test scripts via UI, API, or CI/CD pipelines.

nodes.name: Scheduler
nodes.notes: Manages automated execution frequency for synthetic tests.

nodes.name: Job Queue
nodes.notes: Buffers scheduled test jobs for execution.

nodes.name: Monitor API
nodes.notes: Serves jobs and receives metrics from private monitors.

nodes.name: Results Queue
nodes.notes: Stores test results for alerting and dashboard visualization.

nodes.name: Public Monitors
nodes.notes: New Relic-managed containerized runtimes for public testing.

nodes.name: Global Accelerator
nodes.notes: Provides static IPs for secure routing from private minions.

nodes.name: Private Monitors
nodes.notes: Customer-hosted containerized minions running inside private networks.

nodes.name: Private Endpoint
nodes.notes: Customer's internal site or API behind firewalls.

edges.notes: Passes job data to monitor API.
